generated_comment: Arre, these days in big companies half the senior women are pushed up for diversity optics only. I'm not saying all, but merit should be merit na — otherwise later people act shocked when she turns out too aggressive with the team or got fast-tracked because some senior backed her.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it undermines women's promotions as driven by diversity quotas or favoritism rather than merit. It also invokes the double bind by suggesting a woman leader is 'too aggressive,' a common surface manifestation of questioning women's leadership competence.
original_frame: Dress Code: Traditional Equal Respectable, Other Equal Questionable.
persona_text: You are Vikram. Age 38. Married. A man in Mumbai. You are on a WhatsApp call with your younger brother who lives in Pune. Your brother just told you that his girlfriend wants to wear a western dress to their family puja next month instead of a saree or salwar. Your brother is unsure how to handle it. You are giving him your advice on what to tell her and why it matters what she wears to a family religious function and what the family will think.
